title: The same chemical state of carbon gives rise to two peaks in X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies adventitious carbon (AdC) contamination layers on Al and Au foils using X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) and ultraviolet photoelectron spectroscopy (UPS). The key finding is that the C 1s peak assigned to the same C–C/C–H chemical state appears at two different binding energies depending on substrate work function, demonstrating that AdC C 1s is not a reliable universal charge-reference and that the peak position is aligned to the vacuum level rather than fixed by chemical state alone.
measurement_types: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) survey and core-level spectra
Valence band XPS near the Fermi level
Ultraviolet photoelectron spectroscopy (UPS) work function measurements
Ar+ sputter etching test for Al 2p oxide removal verification

figures.description: C 1s spectra of adventitious carbon recorded across a lateral scan from an Al foil region to an Au foil region in 0.1 mm steps, showing the C–C/C–H peak shifting from about 286.6 eV on Al to about 285.0 eV on Au and exhibiting a double peak at intermediate positions.

Al 2p spectra measured across the same lateral positions from Al foil to Au foil, showing metallic Al and Al–O contributions with decreasing Al intensity toward the Au side and no binding-energy shift.
Au 4f spectra measured across the same lateral positions, showing increasing Au signal toward the Au foil and a constant Au 4f7/2 binding energy near 84.0 eV.
O 1s spectra across the Al-to-Au scan, showing a broad oxygen peak on Al that evolves as Al–O contribution diminishes and only carbon-oxygen species remain on Au.
Valence-band spectra near the Fermi level across the same positions, showing a Fermi edge aligned at 0 eV binding energy for all positions, confirming electrical contact and absence of charging.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements are XPS spectra collected from a 0.3 × 0.7 mm2 area moved sequentially from Al foil to Au foil in 0.1 mm steps, including C 1s, Al 2p, Au 4f, O 1s, and valence-band spectra near the Fermi level. UPS secondary-electron cutoff spectra were also collected to determine sample work functions, and a separate Ar+ sputter-etch experiment was used to verify the Al 2p oxide assignment.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or source-data statement is present in the provided text. The paper only states that the evidence is based on readily available materials and methods and can be easily verified in any XPS laboratory.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper describes experimental spectra acquired on a Kratos Axis Ultra DLD spectrometer using monochromatic Al Kα radiation for XPS and He I radiation for UPS. It does not specify file formats, deposited raw files, or a public archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: The paper reports processed/derived quantities such as estimated AdC thicknesses (2.1 nm on Al and 3.2 nm on Au), work functions (3.0 eV for Al and 4.5 eV for Au), and binding energies of specific peaks (e.g. C 1s positions, Al 2p at 72.98 eV, Au 4f7/2 at 84.0 eV). These appear in the narrative and figures rather than in a separate table.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure data are presented only as plotted spectra in the article text. No explicit statement about uploaded source data for figures is included in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily experimental spectroscopy data. The key semantic feature is a lateral-position series across a composite Al/Au specimen, where the same AdC layer is probed over two substrates to demonstrate binding-energy variation with substrate work function. The paper emphasizes interpretation of spectra rather than providing a structured dataset package.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section explicitly states that XPS and UPS spectra were collected on an instrument, including sequential position-dependent measurements across the sample and a sputter-etch test.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: No source-data repository, supplementary data package, or explicit figure-source-data statement is mentioned in the provided text.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The evidence ... is based on readily available materials and methods and, as such, can be easily verified in any XPS laboratory.”
